classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XLV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm, even as we ourselves have been singing with gladness together with you, we would beg you in like manner to consider with attention together with us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is sung of the sacred Marriage-feast; of the Bridegroom and the Bride; of the King and His people; of the Saviour and those who are to be saved.…His sons are we, in that we are the “children of the Bridegroom;” and it is to us that this Psalm is addressed, whose title has the words, “For the sons of Korah, for the things that shall be changed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why need I explain what is meant by, “for the things that shall be changed”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every one who is himself “changed,” recognises the meaning of this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him who hears this, “for the things that shall be changed,” consider what was before, and what is now.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And first let him see the world itself to be changed, lately worshipping idols, now worshipping God; lately serving things that they themselves made, now serving Him by whom they themselves were made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe at what time the words, “for the things that shall be changed,” were said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Already by this time the Pagans that are left are in dread of the “changed” state of things: and those who will not suffer themselves to be “changed” see the churches full; the temples deserted; see crowds here, and there solitude!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They marvel at the things so changed; let them read that they were foretold; let them lend their ears to Him who promised it; let them believe Him who fulfils that promise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But each one of us, brethren, also undergoes a change from “the old” to “the new man:” from an infidel to a believer: from a thief to a giver of alms: from an adulterer to a man of chastity; from an evildoer to a doer of good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To us then be sung the words, “for the things that shall be changed;” and so let the description of Him by whom they were changed, begin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it goes on, “For the things that shall be changed, to the sons of Korah for understanding; a song for the beloved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that “beloved” One was seen by His persecutors, but yet not for “understanding.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “had they known Him, they would never have crucified the Lord of Glory.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In order to this “understanding,” other eyes were required by Him when He said, “He that seeth Me, seeth My Father also.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Psalm then now sound of Him, let us rejoice in the marriage-feast, and we shall be with those of whom the marriage is made, who are invited to the marriage; and the very persons invited are the Bride herself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Church is “the Bride,” Christ the Bridegroom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are commonly spoken by balladists certain verses to Bridegrooms and Brides, called Epithalamia.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever is sung there, is sung in honour of the Bride and Bridegroom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is there then no Bridechamber in that marriage-feast to which we are invited?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence then does another Psalm say, “He hath set up His tabernacle in the Sun; and He is even as a bridegroom coming out of his chamber.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The nuptial union is that of “the Word,” and the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Bridechamber of this union, the Virgin’s womb.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the flesh itself was united to the Word: whence also it is said, “Henceforth they are not twain, but one flesh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church was assumed unto Him out of the human race: so that the Flesh itself, being united to the Word, might be the Head of the Church: and the rest who believe, members of that Head.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine heart hath uttered a good word” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is the speaker?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Father, or the Prophet?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For some understand it to be the Person of the Father, which says, “Mine heart hath uttered a good word,” intimating to us a certain unspeakable generation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lest you should haply think something to have been taken unto Him, out of which God should beget the Son (just as man takes something to himself out of which he begets children, that is to say, an union of marriage, without which man cannot beget offspring), lest then you should think that God stood in need of any nuptial union, to beget “the Son,” he says, “Mine heart hath uttered a good word.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This very day thine heart, O man, begets a counsel, and requires no wife: by the counsel, so born of thine heart, thou buildest something or other, and before that building subsists, the design subsists; and that which thou art about to produce, exists already in that by which thou art going to produce it; and thou praisest the fabric that as yet is not existing, not yet in the visible form of a building, but on the projecting of a design: nor does any one else praise thy design, unless either thou showest it to him, or he sees what thou hast done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then by the Word “all things were made,” and the Word is of God, consider the fabric reared by the Word, and learn from that building to admire His counsels!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What manner of Word is that by which heaven and earth were made; and all the splendour of the heavens; all the fertility of the earth; the expanse of the sea; the wide diffusion of air; the brightness of the constellations; the light of sun and moon?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are visible things: rise above these also; think of the Angels, “Principalities, Thrones, Dominions, and Powers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All were made by Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then were these good things made?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because there was “uttered forth ‘a good Word,’” by which they were to be made.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It proceeds: “I speak of the things which I have made unto the King.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is the Father still speaking?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the Father is still speaking, let us enquire how this also can be understood by us, consistently with the true Catholic Faith, “I speak of the things that I have made unto the King.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if it is the Father speaking of His own works to His Son, our “King,” what works is the Father to speak of to the Son, seeing that all the Father’s works were made by the Son’s agency?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or, in the words, “I speak of My works unto the King,” does the word, “I speak,” itself signify the generation of the Son?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I fear whether this can ever be made intelligible to those slow of comprehension: I will nevertheless say it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let those who can follow me, do so: lest if it were left unsaid, even those who can follow should not be able.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have read where it is said in another Psalm, “God hath spoken once.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So often has He spoken by the Prophets, so often by the Apostles, and in these days by His Saints, and does He say, “God has spoken once”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How can He have spoken but “once,” except with reference to His “Word”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But as the “Mine heart hath uttered a good Word,” was understood by us in the other clause of the generation of the Son, it seems that a kind of repetition is made in the following sentence, so that the “Mine heart hath uttered a good Word,” which had been already said, is repeated in what He is now saying, “I speak.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what does “I speak” mean?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I utter a Word.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence but from His heart, from His very inmost, does God utter the Word?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You yourself do not speak anything but what you bring forth from your “heart,” this word of yours which sounds once and passes away, is brought forth from no other place: and do you wonder that God “speaks” in this manner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But God’s “speaking” is eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You are speaking something at the present moment, because you were silent before: or, look you, you have not yet brought forth your word; but when you have begun to bring it forth, you as it were “break silence;” and bring into being a word, that did not exist before.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was not so God begat the “Word.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God’s “speaking” is without beginning, and without end: and yet the “Word” He utters is but “One.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Him utter another, if what He has spoken shall have passed away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But since He by whom it is uttered abideth, and That which is uttered abideth; and is uttered but once, and has no end, that very “once” too is said without beginning, and there is no second speaking, because that which is said once, does not pass away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The words “Mine heart hath uttered a good Word,” then, are the same thing with, “I speak of the things which I have made unto the King.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then, “I speak of the things which I have made”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because in the Word Itself are all the works of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whatever God designed to make in the creation already existed in “the Word;” and would not exist in the reality, had it not existed in the Word, just as with you the thing would not exist in the building, had it not existed in your design: even as it is said in the Gospel: “That which was made in Him was life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which was made then was in existence; but it had its existence in the Word: and all the works of God existed there, and yet were not as yet “works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Word” however already was, as this “Word was God, and was with God:” and was the Son of God, and One God with the Father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I speak of the things I have made unto the King.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him hear Him “speaking,” who apprehends “the Word:” and let him see together with the Father the Everlasting Word; in whom exist even those things that are yet to come: in whom even those things that are past have not passed away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These “works” of God are in “the Word,” as in the Word, as in the Only-Begotten, as in the “Word of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What follows then?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My tongue is the pen of a writer writing rapidly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What likeness, my brethren, what likeness, I ask, has the “tongue” of God with a transcriber’s pen?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What resemblance has “the rock” to Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What likeness does the “lamb” bear to our Saviour, or what “the lion” to the strength of the Only-Begotten?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet such comparisons have been made; and were they not made, we should not be formed to a certain extent by these visible things to the knowledge of the “Invisible One.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then with this mean simile of the pen; let us not compare it to His excellent greatness, so let us not reject it with contempt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I ask, why He compares His “tongue” to “the pen of a writer writing rapidly”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how swiftly soever the transcriber writes, still it is not comparable to that swiftness of which another Psalm says, “His word runneth very swiftly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it appears to me (if human understanding may presume so far) that this too may be understood as spoken in the Person of the Father: “My tongue is the pen of a writer.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Inasmuch as what is spoken by the “tongue,” sounds once and passes away, what is written, remains; seeing then that God uttereth “a Word,” and the Word which is uttered does not sound once and pass away, but is uttered and yet continues, God chose rather to compare this to words written than to sounds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what He added, saying, “of one writing swiftly,” stimulates the mind unto “understanding.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let it however not slothfully rest here, thinking of transcribers, or thinking of some kind of quick shorthand writers: if it be this it sees in the passage, it will be resting there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let it think swiftly what is the meaning of that word “swiftly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The “swiftly” of God is such that nothing exceeds in swiftness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in writings letter is written after letter; syllable after syllable; word after word: nor do we pass to the second except when the first is written out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there nothing can exceed the swiftness, where there are not several words; and yet there is not anything omitted: since in the One are contained all things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo! now then that Word, so uttered, Eternal, the Co-eternal Offspring of the Eternal, will come as “the Bridegroom;” “Fairer than the children of men” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Than the children of men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I ask, why not than the Angels also?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why did he say, “than the children of men,” except because He was Man?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lest you should think “the Man Christ” to be any ordinary man, he says, “Fairer than the children of men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even though Himself “Man,” He is “fairer than the children of men;” though among the children of men, “fairer than the children of men:” though of the children of men, “fairer than the children of men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Grace is shed abroad on Thy lips.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Law was given by Moses.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Grace and Truth came by Jesus Christ.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There have not been wanting those who preferred understanding all the preceding passage also of the Prophet’s own person; and would have even this verse, “Mine heart hath uttered forth a good word,” understood as spoken by the Prophet, supposed to be uttering a hymn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whoever utters a hymn to God, his heart is, as it were, “uttering forth a good word,” just as his heart who blasphemes God, is uttering forth an evil word.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So that even by what follows, “I speak of the things which I have made unto the King,” he meant to express that man’s chief work was but to praise God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To Him it belongs to satisfy thee, by His beauty; to thee to praise Him with thanksgiving.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My tongue is the pen of a writer writing quickly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There have been persons who have understood the Prophet to have been describing in this manner what he was writing; and therefore to have compared his tongue to “the pen of a writer writing quickly:” but that he chose to express himself in the words “writing quickly,” to signify, that he was writing of things which were to come “quickly;” that “writing quickly” should be understood to be equivalent to “writing things that are quick;” i.e. writing things that would not long tarry.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For God did not tarry long to manifest Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How quickly is that perceived to have rolled by, which is acknowledged to be already past!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Call to mind the generations before thee; thou wilt find that the making of Adam is but a thing of yesterday.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So do we read that all things have gone on from the very beginning: they were therefore done “quickly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The day of Judgment also will be here “quickly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do thou anticipate its “quick” coming.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is to come “quickly;” do thou become converted yet more “quickly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Judge’s face will appear: but observe thou what the Prophet says, “Let us come before” (let us “prevent”) “His face with confession.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Gird Thy sword upon Thy thigh, O most Mighty” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by “Thy sword,” but “Thy word”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was by that sword He scattered His enemies; by that sword he divided the son from the father, “the daughter from the mother, the daughter-in-law from the mother-in-law.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We read these words in the Gospel, “I came not to send peace, but a sword.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And, “In one house shall five be divided against each other; three against two, and two against three;” i.e. “the father against the son, the daughter against the mother, the daughter-in-law against the mother-in-law.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By what “sword,” but that which Christ brought, was this division wrought?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And indeed, my brethren, we see this exemplified daily.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some young man is minded to give himself up to God’s service; his father is opposed to it; they are “divided against each other:” the one promises an earthly inheritance, the other loves an heavenly; the one promises one thing, the other prefers another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The father should not think himself wronged: God alone is preferred to him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And yet he is at strife with the son, who would fain give himself to God’s service.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the spiritual sword is mightier to separate them, than the ties of carnal nature to bind them together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This happens also in the case of a mother against her daughter; still more also in that of a daughter-in-law against a mother-in-law.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For sometimes in one house mother-in-law and daughter-in law are found orthodox and heretical respectively.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And where that sword is forcibly felt, we do not dread the repetition of Baptism.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Could daughter be divided against mother; and could not daughter-in-law be divided against mother-in-law?…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What does he mean to express by the “thigh”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence those words, “A prince shall not depart from Judah; and a lawgiver from his thighs”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did not Abraham himself (to whom was promised the seed in which “all the nations of the earth were to be blessed”), when he sent his servant to seek and to bring home a wife for his son, being by faith fully persuaded, that in that, so to speak, contemptible seed was contained the great Name; that is, that the Son of God was to come of the seed of Abraham, out of all the children of men; did not he, I say, cause his servant to swear unto him in this manner, saying, “Put thy hand under my thigh,” and so swear; as if he had said, “Put thy hand on the altar, or on the Gospel, or on the Prophet, or on any holy thing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Put” (he says) “thy hand under my thigh;” having full confidence, not ashamed of it as unseemly, but understanding therein a truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With Thy beauty and Thy glory.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take to Thee that righteousness, in which Thou art at all times beautiful and glorious.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And speed on, and proceed prosperously, and reign” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do we not see it so?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it not already come to pass?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He has “sped on; has proceeded prosperously, and He reigns;” all nations are subdued unto Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What a thing was it to see that “in the Spirit,” of which same thing it is now in our power to experience in the reality!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At the time when these words were said, Christ did not yet “reign” thus; had not yet sped on, nor “proceeded prosperously.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were then being preached, they have now been fulfilled: in many things we have God’s promise fulfilled already; in some few we have to claim its fulfilment yet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Because of truth, meekness, and righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truth was restored unto us, when “the Truth sprung out of the earth: and Righteousness looked out from heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ was presented to the expectation of mankind, that in Abraham’s Seed “all nations should be blessed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Gospel has been preached.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is “the Truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by “meekness”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Martyrs have suffered; and the kingdom of God has made much progress from thence, and advanced throughout all nations; because the Martyrs suffered, and neither “fell away,” nor yet offered resistance; confessing everything, concealing nothing; prepared for everything, shrinking from nothing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Marvellous “meekness”!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This did the body of Christ, by its Head it learned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was first “led as a sheep to the slaughter, and as a lamb before his shearer is dumb, even so opened not His mouth;” meek to that degree, that while hanging on the Cross, He said, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why because of “righteousness”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will come also to judge, and to “render to every man according to his works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He spake “the truth;” He patiently endured unrighteousness: He is to bring “righteousness” hereafter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thy right hand shall lead Thee on marvellously.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We shall be guided on by His right hand: He by His own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He is God, we mortal men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was led on by His own right hand; i.e. by His own power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the power which the Father hath, He hath also; the Father’s immortality He hath also; He hath the Father’s Divinity, the Father’s Eternity, the Father’s Power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Marvellously will His right hand lead Him on, performing the works of God; undergoing human sufferings, overthrowing the evil wills of men by His own goodness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even now, He is being led on even to places where as yet He is not; and it is His own right hand that is leading Him on.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that is leading Him thither which He has Himself bestowed upon His Saints.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy right hand shall lead Thee on marvellously.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thine arrows are sharp, are most powerful” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5); words that pierce the heart, that kindle love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence in the Song of Songs it is said, “I am wounded with love.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For she speaks of being “wounded with love;” that is, of being in love, of being inflamed with passion, of sighing for the Bridegroom, from whom she received the arrow of the Word.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thine arrows are sharp, are most powerful;” both piercing, and effective; “sharp, most powerful.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The peoples shall fall under Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who have “fallen”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who were “wounded” have also “fallen.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We see the nations subdued unto Christ; we do not see them “fall.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He explains where they “fall,” viz. “in the heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was there they lifted themselves up against Christ, there they “fall” down before Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Saul was a blasphemer of Christ: he was then lifted up, he prays to Christ, “he is fallen,” he is prostrate before Him: the enemy of Christ is slain, that the disciple of Christ may live!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By an arrow launched from heaven, Saul (not as yet Paul, but still Saul), still lifted up, still not yet prostrate, is wounded in “the heart:” he received the arrow, he fell “in heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For though he fell prostrate on his face, it was not there that he fell down in heart: but it was there where he said aloud, “Lord, what dost Thou bid me do?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But just now thou wert going to bind the Christians, and to bring them to punishment: and now thou sayest unto Christ, “What dost Thou bid me do?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O arrow sharp and most mighty, by whose stroke “Saul” fell, so as to become “Paul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As it was with him, so was it also with “the peoples;” consider the nations, observe their subjection unto Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The peoples” (then) “shall fall under Thee in the heart of the King’s enemies;” that is, in the heart of Thine enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is Him that he calls King, Him that he recognises as King.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The peoples shall fall under Thee in the heart of the King’s enemies.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were “enemies” before; they have been stricken by thine arrows: they have fallen before Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Out of enemies they have been made friends: the enemies are dead, the friends survive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the meaning of, “for those which shall be changed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We are seeking to “understand” each single word, and each separate verse; yet so far only are we to seek for their “understanding,” as to leave no one to doubt that they are spoken of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because God has “‘blessed Thee’ for ever,” on account of the “grace poured over Thy lips.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the throne of the Jewish Kingdom was a temporal one; belonging to those who were under the Law, not to those who were under “grace:” He came to “redeem those who were under the Law,” and to place them under “Grace.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His “Throne is for ever and ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why? for that first throne of the Kingdom was but a temporal one: whence then have we a “throne for ever and ever”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it is God’s throne.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O divine Attribute of Eternity! for God could not have a temporal throne.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy throne, O God, is for ever and ever—a sceptre of direction is the sceptre of Thy Kingdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The sceptre of direction” is that which directs mankind: they were before crooked, distorted; they sought to reign for themselves: they loved themselves, loved their own evil deeds: they submitted not their own will to God; but would fain have bent God’s will to conformity with their own lusts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the sinner and the unrighteous man is generally angry with God, because it rains not! and yet would have God not be angry with himself, because he is profligate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And it is pretty much for this very reason that men daily sit, to dispute against God: “This is what He ought to have done: this He has not well done.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou forsooth seest what thou doest; He knows not what He does!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is thou that art crooked!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His ways are right.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When wilt thou make the crooked coincide with the straight?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It cannot be made to coincide with it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just as if you were to place a crooked stick on a level pavement; it does not join on to it; it does not cohere; it does not fit into the pavement.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The pavement is even in every part: but that is crooked; it does not fit into that which is level.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The will of God then is “equal,” thine own is “crooked:” it is because thou canst not be conformed unto it, that it seems “crooked” unto thee: rule thou thyself by it; seek not to bend it to thine own will: for thou canst not accomplish it; that is at all times “straight”!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wouldest thou abide in Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Correct thou thyself;” so will the sceptre of Him who rules thee, be unto thee “a rule of direction.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence is He also called King, from “ruling.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that is no “ruler” that does not correct.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hereunto is our King a King of “right ones.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just as He is a Priest (Sacerdos) by sanctifying us, so is He our King, our Ruler, by “ruling” us.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated iniquity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See there “the rod of direction” described.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated iniquity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Draw near to that “rod;” let Christ be thy King: let Him “rule” thee with that rod, not crush thee with it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that rod is “a rod of iron;” an inflexible rod.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt rule them with a rod of iron: and break them in pieces like a potter’s vessel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some He rules; others He “breaks in pieces:” He “rules” them that are spiritual: He “breaks in pieces” them that are carnal.…Would He so loudly declare that He was about to smite thee, if He wished to smite thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is then holding back His hand from the punishment of thine offences; but do not thou hold back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Turn thou thyself to the punishment of thine offences: for unpunished offences cannot be: punishment therefore must be executed either by thyself, or by Him: do thou then plead guilty, that He may reprieve thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider an instance in that penitential Psalm: “Hide Thy face from my sins.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did he mean “from me”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No: for in another passage he says plainly, “Hide not Thy face from me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turn” then “Thy face from my sins.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I would have Thee not see my sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For God’s “seeing” is animadverting upon.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence too a Judge is said to “animadvert” on that which he punishes; i.e. to turn his mind on it, to bend it thereon, even to the punishment of it, inasmuch as he is the Judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So too is God a Judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turn Thou Thy face from my sins.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou thyself, if thou wouldest have God turn “His face” from them, turn not thine own face from them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe how he proposes this to God in that very Psalm: “I acknowledge,” he says, “my transgression, and my sin is ever before me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He would fain have that which he wishes to be ever before his own eyes, not be before God’s eyes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no one flatter himself with fond hopes of God’s mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His sceptre is “a sceptre of righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do we say that God is not merciful?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What can exceed His mercy, who shows such forbearance to sinners; who takes no account of the past in all that turn unto Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So love thou Him for His mercy, as still to wish that He should be truthful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For mercy cannot strip Him of His attribute of justice: nor justice of that of mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Meanwhile during the time that He postpones thy punishment, do not thou postpone it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Therefore, God, Thy God, hath anointed Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was for this reason that He anointed thee, that thou mightest love righteousness, and hate iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And observe in what way he expresses himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Therefore, God, Thy God, hath anointed Thee:” i.e. “God hath anointed Thee, O God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God” is “anointed” by God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the Latin it is thought to be the same case of the noun repeated: in the Greek however there is a most evident distinction; one being the name of the Person addressed; and one His who makes the address, saying, “God hath anointed Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, Thy God hath anointed Thee,” just as if He were saying, “Therefore hath Thy God, O God, anointed Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take it in that sense, understand it in that sense; that such is the sense is most evident in the Greek.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who then is the God that is “anointed” by God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Jews tell us; these Scriptures are common to us and them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was God, who was anointed by God: you hear of an “Anointed” one; understand it to mean “Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the name of “Christ” comes from “chrism;” this name by which He is called “Christ” expresses “unction:” nor were kings and prophets anointed in any kingdom, in any other place, save in that kingdom where Christ was prophesied of, where He was anointed, and from whence the Name of Christ was to come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is found nowhere else at all: in no one nation or kingdom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God, then, was anointed by God; with what oil was He anointed, but a spiritual one?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the visible oil is in the sign, the invisible oil is in the mystery; the spiritual oil is within.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God” then was “anointed” for us, and sent unto us; and God Himself was man, in order that He might be “anointed:” but He was man in such a way as to be God still.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He was God in such a way as not to disdain to be man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Very man and very God;” in nothing deceitful, in nothing false, as being everywhere true, everywhere “the Truth” itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God then is man; and it was for this cause that “God” was “anointed,” because God was Man, and became “Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This was figured in Jacob’s placing a stone at his head, and so sleeping.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The patriarch Jacob had placed a stone at his head: sleeping with that stone at his head, he saw heaven opened, and a ladder from heaven to earth, and Angels ascending and descending; after this vision he awaked, anointed the stone, and departed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that “stone” he understood Christ; for that reason he anointed it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take notice what it is whereby Christ is preached.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the meaning of that anointing of a stone, especially in the case of the Patriarchs who worshipped but One God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was however done as a figurative act: and he departed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he did not anoint the stone, and come to worship there constantly, and to perform sacrifice there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was the expression of a mystery; not the commencement of sacrilege.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And notice the meaning of “the stone.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Stone which the builders refused, this is become the head of the corner.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Notice here a great mystery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The “Stone” is Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Peter calls Him “a living Stone, disallowed indeed of men, but chosen of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the stone is set at “the head,” because “Christ is the Head of the man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And “the stone” was anointed, because “Christ” was so called from His being anointed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in the revelation of Christ, the ladder from earth to heaven is seen, or from heaven to earth, and the Angels ascending and descending.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What this means, we shall see more clearly, when we have quoted the testimony from the Lord Himself in the Gospel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You know that Jacob is the same as Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when he wrestled with the Angel, and “prevailed,” and had been blest by Him over whom he prevailed, his named was changed, so that he was called “Israel;” just as the people of Israel “prevailed” against Christ, so as to crucify Him, and nevertheless was (in those who believed in Christ) blest by Him over whom it prevailed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But many believed not; hence the halting of Jacob.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here we have at once, blessing and halting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Blessing on those who became believers; for we know that afterward many of that people did believe: Halting on the other hand in those who believed not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because the greater part believed not, and but few believed, therefore that a halting might be produced, He touched “the breadth of his thigh.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by the breadth of the thigh?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The great multitude of his descendants.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God, Thy God, hath anointed Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have been speaking of God, who was “anointed;” i.e. of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The name of Christ could not be more clearly expressed than by His being called “God the Anointed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the same way in which He was “beautiful before the children of men,” so is He here “anointed with the oil of gladness above His fellows.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who then are His “fellows”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The children of men; for that He Himself (as the Son of Man) became partaker of their mortality in order to make them partakers of His Immortality.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Out of Thy garments is the smell of myrrh, amber, and cassia” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Out of Thy garments is perceived the smell of fragrant odours.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By His garments are meant His Saints, His elect, His whole Church, which he shows forth, as His garment, so to speak; His robe “without spot and wrinkle,” which on account of its spots He has “washed” in His blood; on account of its “wrinkles” extended on His Cross.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence the sweet savour which is signified by certain perfumes there mentioned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear Paul, that “least of the Apostles” (that “hem of that garment,” which the woman with the issue of blood touched, and was healed), hear him saying: “We are a sweet savour of Christ, in every place, both in them that are saved, and in them that perish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He did not say, “We are a sweet savour in them that are saved, and a foul savour in them that are lost:” but, as far as relates to ourselves, “we are a sweet savour both in them that are saved, and in them that perish.”…They who loved him were saved by the odour of “sweet savour;” they who envied him, perished by means of that “sweet savour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To them that perished then he was not a foul “savour,” but a “sweet savour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it was for this very reason they the more envied him, the more excellent that grace was which reigned in him: for no man envies him who is unhappy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then was glorious in the preaching of God’s Word, and in regulating his life according to the rule of that “rod of direction;” and he was loved by those who loved Christ in him, who followed after and pursued the odour of sweet savour; who loved the friend of the bridegroom: that is to say, by the Bride Herself, who says in the Song of Songs, “We will run after the sweet savour of thy perfumes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the others, the more they beheld him invested with the glory of the preaching of the Gospel, and of an irreproachable life, were so much the more tortured with envy, and found that sweet savour prove death to them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Out of thy ivory palaces, whereby kings’ daughters have made Thee glad.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Choose whichever you please, “ivory” palaces, or “magnificent,” or “royal” palaces, it is out of these that the kings’ daughters have made Christ glad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would you understand the spiritual sense of “ivory palaces”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Understand by them the magnificent houses, and tabernacles of God, the hearts of the Saints; and by these self-same “kings” those who rule their flesh; who bring into subjection to themselves the rebellious commonalty of human affections, who chastise the body, and reduce it to bondage: for it is from these that the daughters of kings have made Him glad.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all the souls that have been born through their preaching and evangelizing are “daughters of kings:” and the Churches, as the daughters of Apostles, are daughters of kings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He is “King of kings;” they themselves kings, of whom it was said, “Ye shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They preached the “Word of Truth;” and begat Churches not for themselves, but for Him.…Therefore as “raising up seed to their brother,” to as many as they begat, they gave the name not of “Paulians” or “Petrians,” but of “Christians.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe whether that sense is not wakefully kept in these verses.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when he said, “out of the ivory palaces, he spake of mansions royal, ample, honourable, peaceful, like the heart of the Saints; he added, “Whereby the kings’ daughters have made Thee glad in Thine honour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are indeed daughters of kings, daughters of thine Apostles, but still “in Thine honour:” for they raised up seed to their brother.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence Paul, when he saw those whom he had raised up unto his Brother, running after his own name, exclaimed, “Was Paul crucified for you?”…No; for he says, “Or were ye baptized in the name of Paul?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The daughters of kings have made Thee glad in Thine honour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Keep, hold fast this “in Thine honour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is meant by having “a wedding garment;” seeking His honour, His glory.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Understand moreover by “kings’ daughters” the cities, which were founded by kings, and have received the faith: and out of the ivory palaces (palaces rich, the proud, the lifted up).

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Kings’ daughters have made Thee glad in Thine honour;” in that they sought not the honour of their founders, but have sought Thine honour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Show me at Rome a temple of Romulus held in so great honour as I can show you the Monument of Peter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In Peter, who is honoured but He who died for us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we are followers of Christ, not followers of Peter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And even if we were born from the brother of Him that is dead, yet are we named after the name of Him who is dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We were begotten by the one, but begotten to the other.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, Rome, Carthage, and several other cities are the daughters of kings, and yet have they “made glad the King in His honour:” and all these make up one single Queen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What a nuptial song!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold in the midst of songs full of rejoicing, comes forth the Bride herself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Bridegroom was coming.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was He who was being described: it was on Him all our attention was fixed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Upon Thy right hand did stand the Queen” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She which stands on the left is no Queen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there will be one standing on “the left” also, to whom it will be said, “Go into everlasting fire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But she shall stand on the right hand, to whom it will be said, “Come, ye blessed of My Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On Thy right hand did stand the Queen, “in a vesture of gold, clothed about with divers colours.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the vesture of this Queen?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is one both precious, and also of divers colours: it is the mysteries of doctrine in all the various tongues: one African, one Syrian, one Greek, one Hebrew, one this, and one that; it is these languages that produce the divers colours of this vesture.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But just as all the divers colours of the vesture blend together in the one vesture, so do all the languages in one and the same faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that vesture, let there be diversity, let there be no rent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See we have “understood” the divers colours of the diversity of tongues; and the vesture to refer to unity: but in that diversity itself, what is meant by the “gold”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wisdom itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let there be any diversity of tongues you please, but there is but one “gold” that is preached of: not a different gold, but a different form of that gold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is the same Wisdom, the same doctrine and discipline that every language preaches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the languages there is diversity; gold in the thoughts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Prophet addresses this Queen (for he delights in singing to her), and moreover each one of us, provided, however, we know where we are, and endeavour to belong to that body, and do belong to it in faith and hope, being united in the membership of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is us whom he addresses, saying, “Hearken, O daughter, and behold” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 10), as being one of the “Fathers” (for they are “daughters of kings”), although it be a Prophet, or although it be an Apostle that is addressing her; addressing her, as a daughter, for we are accustomed to speak in this way, “Our fathers the Prophets, our fathers the Apostles;” if we address them as “fathers,” they may address us as children: and it is one father’s voice addressing one daughter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hearken, O daughter, and see.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Hear” first; afterward “see.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they came to us with the Gospel; and that has been preached to us, which as yet we do not see, and which on hearing of it we believed, which by believing it, we shall come to see: even as the Bridegroom Himself speaks in the Prophet, “A people whom I have not known served me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the hearing of me with the ear it obeyed me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by on “hearing of me with the ear”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That they did not “see.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Jews saw Him, and crucified Him; the Gentiles saw Him not, and believed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the Queen who comes from the Gentiles come in “the vesture of gold, clothed with divers colours;” let her come from among the Gentiles clad in all languages, in the unity of Wisdom: let it be said unto her, “Hearken, O daughter, and see.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou wilt not hear, thou shalt not “see.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And incline thine ear.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is not enough to “hearken;” hearken with humility: bow down thine ear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Forget also thine own people, and thy father’s house.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was a certain “people,” and a certain house of thy father, in which thou wast born, the people of Babylon, having the devil for thy king.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whencesoever the Gentiles came, they came from their father the devil; but they have renounced their sonship to the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Forget also thine own people, and thy father’s house.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He, in making thee a sinner, begat thee loathsome: the Other, in that “He justifies the ungodly,” begetteth thee again in beauty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the King hath greatly desired thy beauty” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What “beauty” is that, save that which is His own work?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Greatly desired the beauty”—Of whom?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of her the sinner, the unrighteous, the ungodly, such as she was with her “father,” the devil, and among her own “people”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No, but hers of whom it is said, “Who is this that cometh up made white?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She was not white then at the first, but was “made” white afterwards.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For “though your sins shall be as scarlet, I will make them white as snow.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The king has greatly desired thy beauty.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What King is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For He is the Lord thy God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now consider whether thou oughtest not to forego that thy father, and thy own people, and to come to this King, who is thy God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy God is “thy King,” thy “King” is also thy Bridegroom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou weddest to thy King, who is thy God: being endowed by Him, being adorned by Him; redeemed by Him, and healed by Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever thou hast, wherewith to be pleasing to Him, thou hast from Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the daughters of Tyre shall worship Him with gifts” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is that selfsame “King, who is thy God,” that the daughters of Tyre shall worship with gifts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The daughters of Tyre are the daughters of the Gentiles; the part standing for the whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Tyre, a city bordering on this country, where the prophecy was delivered, typified the nations that were to believe in Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence came that Canaanitish woman, who was at first called “a dog;” for that ye may know that she was from thence, the Gospel speaks thus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He departed into the parts of Tyre and Sidon, and behold a woman of Canaan came out of the same coasts,” with all the rest that is related there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: She who at first, at the house of her “father,” and among her “own people,” was but “a dog,” who by coming to, and crying after that “King,” was made beautiful by believing in Him, what did she obtain to hear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O woman, great is thy faith.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The King has greatly desired thy beauty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the daughters of Tyre shall worship with gifts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With what gifts?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even so would this King be approached, and would have His treasuries filled: and it is He Himself who has given us that wherewith they may be filled, and may be filled by you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them come (He says) and “worship Him with gifts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is meant by “with gifts”?…“Give alms, and all things are clean unto you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Come with gifts to Him that saith, “I will have mercy rather than sacrifice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To that Temple that existed aforetime as a shadow of that which was to come, they used to come with bulls, and rams, and goats, with every different kind of animal for sacrifice: that with that blood one thing should be done, and another be typified by it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now that very blood, which all these things used to figure, hath come: the King Himself hath come, and He Himself would have your “gifts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He Himself will judge hereafter, and will Himself hereafter account “gifts” to certain persons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Come” (He says), “ye blessed of My Father.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I was an hungred, and ye gave Me meat,” etc. These are the gifts with which the daughters of Tyre worship the King; for when they said, “When saw we Thee?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who is at once above and below (whence those “ascending” and “descending” are spoken of ), said, “Inasmuch as ye have done it unto one of the least of Mine, ye have done it unto Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 26. …“The rich among the people shall entreat Thy face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both they who shall entreat that face, and He whose face they will entreat, are all collectively but one Bride, but one Queen, mother and children belonging all together unto Christ, belonging unto their Head.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All the glory of her, the King’s daughter, is from within” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only is her robe, outwardly, “of gold, and of divers colours;” but He who loved her beauty, knew her to be also beautiful within.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are those inward charms?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those of conscience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is there Christ sees; it is there Christ loves her: it is there He addresses her, there punishes, there crowns.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let then thine alms be done in secret; for “all the glory of her, the King’s daughter, is from within.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With fringes of gold, clothed with divers colours” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Her beauty is from within; yet in the “fringes of gold” is the diversity of languages: the beauty of doctrine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do these avail, if them be not that beauty “from within”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The virgins shall be brought unto the King after her.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It has been fulfilled indeed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church has believed; the Church has been formed throughout all nations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And to what a degree do virgins now seek to find favour in the eyes of that King!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence are they moved to do so?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even because the Church preceded them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The virgins shall be brought unto the King after her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Her near kinswomen shall be brought unto Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they that are brought unto Him are not strangers, but her “near kinswomen,” that belong to her.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because he had said, “unto the King,” he says, turning the discourse to Him, “her near kinswomen shall be brought unto Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With gladness and rejoicing shall they be brought and shall be led into the Temple of the King” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The “Temple of the King” is the Church itself: it is the Church itself that enters into “the Temple of the King.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whereof is that Temple constructed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of the men who enter the Temple?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who but God’s “faithful” ones are its “living stones”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall be led into the Temple of the King.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there are virgins without the Temple of the King, the nuns among the heretics: they are virgins, it is true; but what will that profit them, unless they be led into the “Temple of the King”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The “Temple of the King” is in unity: the “Temple of the King” is not ruinous, is not rent asunder, is not divided.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The cement of those living stones is “charity.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Instead of thy fathers, children are born to thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nothing can be more manifest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now consider the “Temple of the King” itself, for it is on its behalf he speaks, on account of the unity of the body that is spread throughout all the world: for those very persons who have chosen to be virgins, cannot find favour with the King unless they be led into the Temple of the King.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Instead of thy fathers, are thy children born to thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was the Apostles begat thee: they were “sent:” they were the preachers: they are “the fathers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But was it possible for them to be with us in the body for ever?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although one of them said, “I desire to depart, and to be with Christ, which is far better: to abide in the flesh is necessary for your sakes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is true he said this, but how long was it possible for him to remain here?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Could it be till this present time, could it be to all futurity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is the Church then left desolate by their departure?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Instead of thy fathers, children have been born to thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is that?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostles were sent to thee as “fathers,” instead of the Apostles sons have been born to thee: there have been appointed Bishops.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the present day, whence do the Bishops, throughout all the world, derive their origin?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church itself calls them fathers; the Church itself brought them forth, and placed them on the thrones of “the fathers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think not thyself abandoned then, because thou seest not Peter, nor seest Paul: seest not those through whom thou wert born.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Out of thine own offspring has a body of “fathers” been raised up to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Instead of thy fathers, have children been born to thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe how widely diffused is the “Temple of the King,” that “the virgins that are not led to the Temple of the King,” may know that they have nothing to do with that marriage.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou shalt make them princes over all the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the Universal Church: her children have been made “princes over all the earth:” her children have been appointed instead of the “fathers.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let those who are cut off own the truth of this, let them come to the One Body: let them be led into the Temple of the King.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God hath established His Temple everywhere: hath laid everywhere “the foundations of the Prophets and Apostles.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church has brought “forth sons;” has made them “instead of her fathers” to be “princes over all the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall be mindful of thy name in every generation and generation; therefore shall the peoples confess unto Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What does it profit then to “confess” indeed and yet to confess out of “the Temple”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What does it profit to pray, and yet not to pray on the Mount?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I cried,” says he, “unto the Lord with my voice: and He heard me out of His holy hill.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Out of what “hill”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Out of that of which it is said, “A city set upon a hill cannot be hid.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of what “hill”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Out of that hill which Daniel saw “grow out of a small stone, and break all the kingdoms of the earth; and cover all the face of the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There let him pray, who hopes to receive: there let him ask, who would have his prayer heard: there let him confess, who wishes to be pardoned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Therefore shall the peoples confess unto thee for ever, world without end.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in that eternal life it is true indeed there will no longer be the mourning over sins: but yet in the praises of God by that everlasting City which is above, there will not be wanting a perpetual confession of the greatness of that happiness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to that City itself, to which another Psalm sings, “Glorious things are spoken of thee, O City of God,” to her who is the very Bride of Christ, the very Queen, a “King’s daughter, and a King’s consort;”…the peoples shall for this very cause confess even to herself; the hearts of all, now enlightened by perfect charity, being laid bare, and made manifest, that she may know the whole of herself most completely, who here is, in many parts of her, unknown to herself.…
